title: Question: How to set an input [127:0] value from C++?
body:
---


Author Name: **Chad Harrington**
Original Redmine Message: 2166 from https://www.veripool.org



---

I am trying out verilator and it looks promising. I am driving the simulation from my sim_main.cpp and want to set an input called "seed" in the module under test. The input is a wide input of type "input [127:0] seed". I can easily set narrower inputs with:

top->clk = 1;

But how can I set the value of seed? It appears that under the covers, an array of 32bit words is used, but I can't figure out how to set that without getting "not assignable" errors.

Thanks for your help!

Chad
